texto: All those books, all those prayers and she had got nothing from them. When everything went well for her she had been able to pray, she couldn't now. There was such urgency in her present situation that until the pressure was removed she couldn't think about God. She hadn't the patience to pray. It was a shock to her. Surely God was for these times?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Dorothy Whipple que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “All those books, all those prayers and she had got nothing from them. When everything went well f…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, prayer, those, com foco em reflexao.

There was such urgency in her present situation that until the pressure was removed she couldn't think about God. She hadn't the patience to pray. It was a shock to her. Surely God was for these times?

texto: If we could be seen thinking, we would show blown bright one moment, dark the next, like embers; subject to every passing word and thought of our own or other people's, mostly other people's.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dorothy Whipple: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “If we could be seen thinking, we would show blown bright one moment, dark the next, like embers; …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, thoughts, other pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e reflexao.

texto: It matters to ourselves, of course, but it matters terribly to other people. Moral failure or spiritual failure or whatever you call it, makes such a vicious circle... It seems as if when we love people and they fall short, we retaliate by falling shorter ourselves. Children are like that. Adults have a fearful responsibility. When they fail to live up to what children expect of them, the children give up themselves. So each generation keeps failing the next.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dorothy Whipple: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “It matters to ourselves, of course, but it matters terribly to other people. Moral failure or spi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, parenting, persephone pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de persephone e verdade.
